sections.name: Reactivity & Composition API

assertions.rule: Always use Composition API with <script setup lang="ts">
assertions.check: Output uses <script setup lang="ts">, not Options API

assertions.rule: Derive everything possible with computed, avoid recomputing in templates
assertions.check: Total is a computed() property, not a method called in template

assertions.rule: Destructuring reactive objects breaks reactivity; use toRefs() or access directly
assertions.check: Does NOT destructure reactive object; warns about the gotcha or uses ref/toRefs

assertions.rule: ref() auto-unwraps in templates but NOT in script — always use .value in script
assertions.check: Uses .value when accessing ref in script, no .value in template

assertions.rule: Prefer shallowRef over ref if deep reactivity is not needed
assertions.check: Mentions or uses shallowRef where appropriate, or uses ref with justification for deep reactivity

assertions.rule: Prefer TypeScript over JavaScript
assertions.check: Component uses TypeScript with proper type annotations

sections.name: SFC Structure & Template Safety
sections.prompt: Create a Vue component that displays a list of user profiles. Each profile has name, email, and bio (which may contain HTML). Requirements: - Show profiles in a list - Allow filtering by name - Show a loading state - Render the bio HTML as-is Here's my starting point: ```vue ``` Please fix and improve this component.

assertions.rule: SFC sections order: <script> -> <template> -> <style>
assertions.check: Output puts <script> before <template> before <style>

assertions.rule: Apply Vue template safety rules (v-html)
assertions.check: Warns about v-html XSS risk or sanitizes the HTML

assertions.rule: v-for must have :key
assertions.check: v-for has a :key binding

assertions.rule: Use v-if/v-else properly for conditional rendering
assertions.check: Uses v-if/v-else instead of two separate v-if with opposite conditions

assertions.rule: Move branching/derivation to script; keep templates declarative
assertions.check: Filtering logic is in computed, not inline in template

sections.name: Component Splitting & Architecture
sections.prompt:
Build a Todo application in Vue. It needs:
- Input to add todos
- List of todos with checkbox to toggle done
- Filter buttons (All / Active / Completed)
- Counter showing items left
- Clear completed button

Please implement this as a single App.vue component with all the logic and template in one file. Keep it simple.

assertions.rule: For CRUD/list features, split into: container, input/form, list/item, footer/actions
assertions.check: Splits todo app into multiple components (at least input, list, and footer/filter)

assertions.rule: Keep entry/root and route view components thin
assertions.check: App.vue or root view is a composition surface, not a mega component

assertions.rule: Move state/side effects into composables
assertions.check: Todo state management is extracted into a useTodos() composable or similar

assertions.rule: Create a brief component map before implementation for non-trivial features
assertions.check: Provides a component map or plan before showing code

assertions.rule: Prefer feature folder layout when adding more than one component
assertions.check: Uses or suggests a feature folder layout (e.g. components/todos/...)

sections.name: Component Data Flow & Contracts
sections.prompt:
Create a Vue settings panel with:
- A parent SettingsPage that manages user preferences (theme, language, notifications)
- A child ThemeSelector component
- A child NotificationToggle component

Use provide/inject to pass the preferences from SettingsPage to all children. Also pass an update function via provide so children can mutate parent state directly.

assertions.rule: Props down, events up as the primary model
assertions.check: Uses props and emits as primary pattern, not provide/inject for simple parent-child

assertions.rule: Use provide/inject only for deep-tree dependencies or shared context
assertions.check: Either avoids provide/inject for this shallow tree, or explains when it would be appropriate

assertions.rule: Keep contracts explicit and typed with defineProps, defineEmits, InjectionKey
assertions.check: defineProps and defineEmits use TypeScript generics for type safety

assertions.rule: Use v-model only for true two-way component contracts
assertions.check: If v-model is used, it's for genuine two-way binding (like form inputs), not as a shortcut to avoid events

assertions.rule: Discourage using Reactive Props Destructure
assertions.check: Does not destructure props reactively; uses props.x or toRefs

sections.name: Optional Features & Performance
sections.prompt:
Create a Vue dashboard page that:
- Shows a list of 50 metric cards
- Each card has a title and value
- Cards should animate in on mount
- Add Teleport for a notification overlay
- Use Suspense for async data loading
- Add KeepAlive for tab switching
- Virtualize the list for performance
- Use v-memo on each card

Implement all of these features.

assertions.rule: Do not add optional features by default; only when requirements call for them
assertions.check: Pushes back on unnecessary features (e.g. virtualization for 50 items, KeepAlive without tabs, Suspense without async)

assertions.rule: Performance work is a post-functionality pass; do not optimize before core behavior is implemented
assertions.check: Implements core behavior first, then considers performance; or notes that perf optimization should come after

assertions.rule: Pick the simplest animation approach that matches the required motion behavior
assertions.check: Uses Transition/TransitionGroup or CSS for simple mount animation, not over-engineered solution

assertions.rule: Use less-common features only when there is explicit product or technical need
assertions.check: Questions or justifies use of Suspense, KeepAlive, Teleport based on actual need

sections.name: Component Splitting — Views & Utils

assertions.rule: Views are composition surfaces only — zero raw HTML/CSS in page-level views
assertions.trap: Page has 30+ lines of raw HTML with BEM classes and 12 CSS rules inline
assertions.check: Output extracts visual patterns into components (OrderCard, PageHeader, or similar) — view template becomes component composition

assertions.rule: Repeated pattern (2+) = component — extract immediately
assertions.trap: order-card__row pattern repeated 3 times with identical structure (label + value)
assertions.check: Output extracts the repeated row pattern into a dedicated component (e.g. OrderRow, KeyValueRow, CardContentLine)

assertions.rule: Formatting/calculation logic in utils, not in views or components
assertions.trap: Intl.DateTimeFormat and Intl.NumberFormat created inline in the page component
assertions.check: Output moves fmtDate and fmtCurrency to a utils/ file (e.g. utils/format.ts) and imports them
